title: design-system.config.json
description: Design system SSOT companion file. Pairs with the stack-specific token file (design-system.config.css / .ts / DesignSystem.cs). This schema validates aesthetic / conventions / antipatterns / tokens metadata.

aesthetic.description: Project's intentional aesthetic commitment. Empty fields here mean the bootstrap interview was skipped — see AESTHETIC.md.

tone.description: One concrete tone. Examples: 'editorial / magazine', 'brutally minimal', 'retro-futuristic / cursed-VHS', 'organic / handmade', 'industrial / utilitarian'. Vague tones like 'modern', 'professional', 'clean', 'minimal' alone are anti-patterns — they describe an absence rather than a commitment.

differentiation.description: The single memorable thing. What does a user describe to a friend the next day? One sentence.

antiDirection.description: Things this project explicitly avoids. The skill recommends 2-5 entries. More entries are fine when the tone is strong (retro-futuristic / brutalist often need many). Should at minimum contradict the easy generic-AI default for this category.

moodKeywords.description: Optional. Vocabulary that anchors the aesthetic for design discussions.

tonePrimary.description: When using mixed tones, the dominant tone. See AESTHETIC.md mixed-tone section.

toneSecondary.description: When using mixed tones, the supporting tone.

toneShifts.description: Per-section/page tone overrides for projects that intentionally shift tone (e.g. landing = playful, blog = editorial).

tokens.description: Optional metadata about the token structure. Actual token values live in the stack-specific file (CSS / TS / C#).

colorStructure.description: How color tokens are arranged. dominant-plus-accent is the recommended default per AESTHETIC.md.

typographyStructure.description: single-family-weights is flagged in IDEAL.md as a generic-AI pattern unless explicitly justified.

motionPolicy.description: Short prose: when motion is used, when it's withheld. e.g. 'one orchestrated hero reveal, all other surfaces static'

decorationStrategy.description: Background / decoration approach. 'solid' should match a minimal aesthetic; otherwise SSOT must define the chosen layer.

antipatterns.description: Project-level prohibitions. The skill flags these specific generic-AI patterns and expects them to appear in this list (or the project explains why they're allowed): Inter / Roboto / Arial / system-ui / Space Grotesk SSOT外使用; 紫グラデ on 白; 中央寄せヒーロー → 3カラム機能 → CTA の定型; solid color にdefaultした背景.

exceptions.description: Documented deviations from IDEAL.md. See deviations format in AESTHETIC.md / DEVIATIONS.md.

rule.description: Which IDEAL.md / AESTHETIC.md rule is being violated

reason.description: Why this is acceptable in this project

reviewBy.description: ISO date by which this deviation should be revisited
